title: Correa lawrenciana var. latrobeana
what_description: Correa lawrenciana var. latrobeana is a plant variety that belongs to the family Rutaceae. It is a small shrub that typically grows up to 1 meter in height, with a spread of approximately 1.5 meters. The leaves are lance-shaped, measuring 6-10 centimeters long and 1-2 centimeters wide, with a glossy green color on the upper surface and a lighter green underside. The plant produces white or cream-colored flowers in late winter, which are tubular in shape and approximately 3-4 centimeters long, with four petals. The flowers are borne in clusters at the end of branches, and the fruit is a capsule that splits to release small, brown seeds.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Correa lawrenciana var. latrobeana are inflammation and anxiety, which are alleviated due to its anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic properties. Its antibacterial and antifungal properties also make it effective in treating digestive issues, skin irritation, wound healing, stress, and insect bites. Furthermore, it has been used to treat depression and fungal infections, demonstrating its broad-spectrum therapeutic potential. Additionally, Correa lawrenciana var. latrobeana has been found to reduce pain and alleviate respiratory problems, as well as infections and diarrhea. Its antiviral properties also make it effective in treating wounds, fever, cold sores, cough, cuts, and respiratory issues.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Correa lawrenciana var. latrobeana are primarily due to its antioxidant properties, which help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. These properties, in turn, contribute to its antimicrobial activity, allowing it to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms and prevent the spread of infections. The bioactive compounds present in this plant, including flavonoids and saponins, also exhibit anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain. Additionally, Correa lawrenciana var. latrobeana contains volatile oils that possess antifungal properties, effective against various fungal pathogens. Furthermore, the plant's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Correa lawrenciana var. latrobeana are flavonoids, specifically quercetin, kaempferol and other polyphenolic compounds, which have been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. The presence of phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, has been identified, contributing to the plant's antioxidant activity and potential in preventing chronic diseases. Furthermore, Correa lawrenciana var. latrobeana contains alkaloids, which are believed to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory and anticancer properties. Research has also isolated flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms and exhibit anti-inflammatory activity. These bioactive compounds have been isolated and identified through various studies, highlighting the plant's potential therapeutic applications.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Correa lawrenciana var. latrobeana are derived from its leaves, which contain flavonoids and phenolic acids. A tincture is made by macerating the leaves in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerol to create a concentrated extract. An infusion is prepared by steeping the leaves in hot water to create a tea that can be used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and digestive problems. A salve is made by infusing the leaves in a carrier oil and then mixing it with a wax to create a topical ointment that can be used to treat skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. These preparations are used due to the plant's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to be effective in traditional medicine.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Correa lawrenciana var. latrobeana improperly include experiencing nausea, causing diarrhea, inducing vomiting, inducing nausea, and developing diarrhea. This is due to the toxic compounds present in the plant, particularly the Correanine alkaloids and Correanine, which can be harmful to humans if ingested. When ingested, these toxic compounds can stimulate the stomach, leading to nausea and vomiting, and also cause irritation in the gastrointestinal tract, resulting in diarrhea. The severity of these side effects can vary depending on the amount and method of ingestion, as well as individual tolerance. If improperly used, Correa lawrenciana var. latrobeana can cause serious health issues, and its use should be strictly regulated and monitored.
